item.title: AI GPUs probably live longer than three years
item.description: People who think current AI use is unsustainable often rely on the claim that inference GPUs only last “three years at the most” under load1. The idea here is that once the AI bubble money drains away, current infrastructure will rapidly become obsolete, and there won’t be enough money floating around to buy a whole slate of brand-new GPUs. Inference costs would thus rapidly become way too expensive for current AI products to make any financial sense.

item.title: GitHub - tamnd/kage: Shadow any website for offline viewing, with the JavaScript stripped out
item.description: Shadow any website for offline viewing, with the JavaScript stripped out - tamnd/kage

item.title: Did Anthropic Ask For This?
item.description: This past Friday, the US Government issued an export control directive that prohibits Anthropic from giving foreign nationals access to Claude Fable or Claude Mythos, their latest models.

item.title: Aim for no
item.description: An ability of successful people is their willingness to aim high, get rejected, and keep going.

item.title: The $75 Billion Bell
item.description: SpaceX rang the largest IPO bell in American history. Folk wisdom says it marks the top. The history is more conditional, and the one clean miss tells you why.

item.title: Testing Uruky to replace Kagi
item.description: I thought that of all of the goals of my European migration journey, Kagi would be the easiest. I believed there to be no alternative, so I could just shrug ...

item.title: Please Stay Calm and Listen - Gibberish and Stuff
item.description: I need to tell you something. Please listen and try not to panic. You've been in a coma for quite some time. Yes, yes, I know, you would like to know how long. I'm afraid it's been... nine...

item.title: The fertile void
item.description: If I'm not being seen in motion, who am I seen as? Am I being lazy? If there is no immediate next mountain, what story do I tell?

item.title: For Retired Engineers…
item.description: I know I’m not the only one to have noticed the fact that many- indeed most – such correspondents are Electrical Engineers (usually retired). I was delighted therefore to see that there…

item.title: No, everyone is not using AI for everything.
item.description: People are consuming AI like they eat meat: some are embracing it, some are limiting their use of it, and some are avoiding it altogether.

item.title: How to Earn a Billion Dollars
item.description: (This is based on a talk I gave at the Oxford Union.)

item.title: ReactOS "Open-Source Windows" Reaches The Milestone Of Being Able To Run Half-Life
item.description: ReactOS, the open-source operating system working for binary compatibility with Microsoft Windows computer programs and drivers, has reached the milestone of being able to enjoy the classic game Half-Life running on this open-source platform.

item.title: SQL to ER Diagram — Free Online ERD Generator from SQL
item.description: Paste a SQL schema and instantly generate a clean, interactive ER diagram. Free, open source, runs in your browser — nothing uploaded.

item.title: Honda Civics and the Evil Valet
AI Coding at Home Without Going Broke
item.description: There are three ways to do AI coding at home without spending like a company, and which one fits depends mostly on how much you trust the next year of hardwa...
